mappings.rationale: A core text on materials processing (A), property modification (A.5), and the science of ceramics, metals, and industrial materials.
Engineering principles underpin the forming (A.2), material removal (A.3), and joining (A.4) processes discussed in this section.
Structural applications like bridges, storage tanks, and ships illustrate welding, brazing, and soldering (B.4).
Chemical processing -- leaching, smelting, polymerization -- is foundational to A.1.b and hydrometallurgy (B.2.c).
Thermodynamic principles govern heat treatment (A.5.a: annealing, stress relieving, hardening) and pyrometallurgy (B.2.a).
Extractive metallurgy (B.2) and the separation of metallic elements from mineral form are central themes.
Crystalline structures in metals during physical metallurgy connect to metallography (B.3.g).
Mineral processing -- crushing and grinding of ores, concentration of metallic minerals -- is central to B.1.
The historical development of metallurgy (B), including iron and steel production techniques, shaped modern materials processing.
Nanoscale dimensions open new possibilities for finishing processes (A.6) and property modification (A.5).
Phase transitions and solid-state physics underlie the heat treatment processes in property modification (A.5).
Radiation exposure for processing (A.5.b.iii) and nuclear engineering metallurgy for uranium (B.3.e) depend on understanding radioactive phenomena.
